title: pedicularis anas var. xanthantha
intro: Pedicularis anas var. xanthantha, also known as the yellow-flowered lousewort, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the borage family and is native to mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, making it useful for treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of Pedicularis anas var. xanthantha include reducing inflammation, relieving coughs, and promoting expectoration, as well as having antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
